comment_text:
So … this isn’t catfishing. It’s outright stalking. Catfishing is faking your identity. Sometimes a catfish can BECOME a stalker, but their goal is to create a relationship (even if it’s a toxic one). Here, the mother was acting as a masked stranger, and her only goal was to terrorize her daughter and Owen. 

Both catfishing and stalking are evil, but there’s also a very important distinction there.

comment_text: I just came here because I know, Kendra, that being such a narcissist, you must be reading this. As an adult woman in my 40s, and as the mom of a girl, I want to tell you that there is NO explanation, let alone justification for what you did. I agree with the police that you started the whole thing, and that you have Münchausen syndrome by proxy, just like Gypsy Rose’s mother. You are sick, very, very sick, and you need permanent psychiatric assistance and an ankle tracking device if not interned into a facility. You broke your daughter permanently, not even mainly by the harassment, but by the discovery of the truth. I also had a sick, mean mother, who needed to control me and all people around her, who had no problem with going to exaggerated extents to achieve that. It really messes with your head that the person who should love you the most, protect you the most, and being the safest and kindest person in your life, is instead a cruel and disturbing monster that you need to keep far, far away from you in order to maintain your mental health. Losing that person that you are supposed to be able to count on, even as an adult, to lean on when life gets too heavy, to get support and empathy, to rest assured, is like a death. I mourning my still alive mother three years ago, when she manipulated me into giving up my part of our family home, then threw me out of it with my daughter, just to either make me stay out of necessity under her terms or eventually returning as my financial situation wasn’t great, so she could humiliate me and take over my life and my daughter’s. Guess what? Three years now, no communication and I couldn’t be happier, as well as my baby. I’ve been through A LOT, A LOT, but mothers like you make us children be willing to endure EVERYTHING when the alternative is to depend on you or to be close to you for any reason. You are going to have very lonely elder elder years, and I’m glad. People like you are poison to whomever is around you. My father died under her ‘care’, negating reality and worshipping her, as she crippled him in all ways so he will depend entirely on her. Thank God that wasn’t my case. Your ex husband did well in separating her from you. At least she has one parent that knows what’s right, and that may be her saving grace. You know, at least you should have been honest, so your daughter could have some peace of mind and stop seeing you as this ambiguous person who we are all biologically inclined to love, instead of the b**ch you really are. Help her dona clean cut on that bond. But, of course, that is not convenient for you, just for her, and we all know that her best interests are the last on your list. Also my mother never admitted any flaws. Never. Too bad you all refuse to do the one good thing you could do for your children: help them to see you as you really are so we can let go for good. Took me four decades to do that process and an additional year to grieve her final loss. I hope it doesn’t take your daughter that much.

video_title: Unknown Number: The High School Catfish | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
A lot of people are wondering why Lauryn doesn’t seem upset at her mom and go no contact, unless you’ve been in a similar situation you don’t know it’s not that easy. Stockholm syndrome, cognitive dissonance, being enmeshed, having a fawn response etc. are all things the victim could possibly be experiencing. To immediately assume someone must have been in on the crimes committed against them, simply because the way they reacted didn’t match what you would’ve done, doesn’t mean they aren’t traumatized by it. She could be in a dissociative state, and that can take years to come out of depending on how bad the trauma was.
You have half the world yelling at you to forgive your parents and “but she’s your mother”, while the other half screams at you to run as far as possible. And when you do what you want without listening to them, you get treated like you’re just as bad as the abuser. Whether a victim runs or stays, someone will always find a way to paint them as the villain. Black sheep, scapegoat, “just like her mother”, “narcissist(for choosing yourself over your abuser)”. 
Even if Lauryn was in on it, let’s be real, what kind of mother would agree to play along? Not a good one

comment_text: When smartphones leapt into the world, they not only changed our lives, they warped some brains.  My kids are grown but we have close friends with teenagers who actually do not have these phones.  They are so happy! The police officer asks Owen's mother, "Did you get him a new phone? Have you taken his phone?" She looks tired and harrassed.... And evades the question. It is like they are staring at a brutal car accident with bodies everywhere and they just can't look away from their addiction....  It is not just that the mother responsible is a psycho sea hag.  She knew the power the phones had over people and used her tech knowledge to stay anonymous and get attention at the same time.  The answer of course is NOT to totally ban phones.  But let's try to detach better and recognize what too much immersion in social media can do to our minds and mental/spiritual health.  Especially the more psychotic and vulnerable minds among us....
